title: Agricultural Scientist
description: An Agricultural Scientist in India spends most of their day conducting research on crop improvement, soil health, and pest management. They work closely with farmers, government agencies, and other stakeholders to develop and implement sustainable agricultural practices. Their work involves collecting and analyzing data, conducting experiments, and communicating their findings to various audiences.
top_govt_colleges: National Institute of Animal Nutrition and Physiology
top_private_colleges: Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel University of Agriculture and Technology
Chandra Shekhar Azad University of Agriculture and Technology

career_progression.year_0_2: Assistants in research institutions, government departments, and private companies. Conduct experiments, collect data, and assist in the development of new agricultural practices.
career_progression.year_3_5: Research assistants in government departments, private companies, and research institutions. Conduct independent research, develop new agricultural practices, and publish research papers.
career_progression.year_6_10: Senior research scientists in government departments, private companies, and research institutions. Lead research teams, develop new agricultural practices, and publish research papers.
senior_paths: Director of Research in a government department
Head of Research in a private company
career_progression.entrepreneurship_exit: Start a company that develops and markets new agricultural products or services.
career_progression.global_pathway: Work in international organizations such as the Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations, or in research institutions in developed countries.

india_social_context.first_year_reality: Starting salary is around ₹4-6 lakhs per annum. Working hours can be long, especially during peak agricultural seasons.
common_misconceptions: Agricultural scientists only work in rural areas
Agricultural scientists only deal with crops
